title: The Fabulous Faker Boy

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's iconic catchphrase as scene opener
jokes.punchline: The familiar exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but no originality at this point in the series

jokes.text: Ew, cheap PVC smell. Oh, no. I'm an action figure! And I hate action.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he's been transformed into an action figure
jokes.punchline: The irony that he hates action despite being an action figure
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific irony - Homer's lazy nature makes the action figure transformation inherently funny

jokes.text: Hiddiley-ho, articulated neighborino. Beautiful day, isn't it? Why, it's practically mint on card.
jokes.setup: Flanders greeting with his typical neighborly enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Adapting his speech patterns to the toy/collectible context with 'articulated neighborino' and 'mint on card'
jokes.explanation: Excellent adaptation of Flanders' speech pattern to the toy context - 'articulated neighborino' is inspired wordplay

jokes.text: Shut up, Flanders!
jokes.setup: Flanders being overly cheerful and neighborly
jokes.punchline: Homer's typical dismissive response to Flanders
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer-Flanders dynamic, reliable but not original

jokes.text: This reality ain't all bad. Pastry power, activate.
jokes.setup: Homer adjusting to toy reality
jokes.punchline: Treating donuts like a superhero power source
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's love of food reimagined as superhero activation

jokes.text: Homer, give me a yank. I'm lonely.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to help with something
jokes.punchline: The unintentionally suggestive phrasing followed by the sad admission
jokes.explanation: Mildly suggestive humor with a touch of pathos

jokes.text: Homer, this isn't the way to the mall. The mall? I thought you said Badass City!
jokes.setup: Marge correcting Homer's navigation
jokes.punchline: Homer's mishearing creates a much cooler destination
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer mishearing joke with good contrast between mundane and exciting

jokes.text: Urgent message from Nelson Muntz. Go on. Haw. Haw.
jokes.setup: Formal announcement of an urgent message
jokes.punchline: The 'urgent message' is just Nelson's signature laugh
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character integration - Nelson's entire personality distilled to his laugh as an 'urgent message'

jokes.text: Let me guess. You need a field trip mom. A library volunteer? Someone to Purell the CPR dummy? Resusci-Kate is just fine, thank you.
jokes.setup: Marge anticipating typical parent volunteer requests
jokes.punchline: Skinner's defensive response about the CPR dummy's condition
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about school volunteer culture, with Skinner's defensive personality shining through

jokes.text: What did he do now? Oh, today, I'm not here to talk about what he did. I'm here to talk about the future. What did he do in the future?
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to have a forward-looking conversation about Bart
jokes.punchline: Marge's assumption that Bart has somehow already caused future trouble
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge so conditioned to Bart's troublemaking that she assumes he's caused future problems

jokes.text: I'm not sure what he's planning, although there is a lot of Internet chatter.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Bart's future behavior
jokes.punchline: Treating a ten-year-old like a terrorist with online surveillance
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of school administrator paranoia about student behavior

jokes.text: less Dennis the Menace, more Casper the Ghost, if he were still alive.
jokes.setup: Skinner wanting to change Bart's character type
jokes.punchline: The logical contradiction of wanting someone to be like a ghost 'if he were still alive'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical contradiction - wanting someone to be like a dead character but alive. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: My head hairs! I'm bald! Get... get... get in there.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering his hair loss
jokes.punchline: His desperate attempt to put the hairs back
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy with Homer's vanity and denial

jokes.text: Did you ever wonder if hippopotamuses think that rhinos are unicorns?
jokes.setup: Homer making random philosophical observation
jokes.punchline: The absurd animal comparison/confusion
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Homer observation with surprising logic - rhinos do kind of look like fat unicorns

jokes.text: If you're worried about your Mother's Day present, it's in the works.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Homer do something more creative
jokes.punchline: Homer completely missing the point and thinking it's about Mother's Day gifts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's obliviousness to deeper meaning

jokes.text: Don't worry, music theory's just a fancy word for music math.
jokes.setup: Trying to make music theory sound approachable
jokes.punchline: Making it sound worse by calling it 'music math'
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - attempt to reassure actually makes it sound more intimidating

jokes.text: My dear boy, you undoubtedly think of the slide whistle as merely a whistle that slides. Let me disabuse you of that misperception.
jokes.setup: Frink about to explain something complex
jokes.punchline: The overly academic approach to a simple novelty instrument
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink character moment - applying academic pomposity to a silly toy

jokes.text: Behold! From the pages of Dune and Dune Messiah, Gurney Halleck's beloved zither: the baliset.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy presenting an instrument
jokes.punchline: The incredibly nerdy reference to obscure sci-fi musical instruments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - incredibly specific nerd reference that only he would make

jokes.text: I see you are wearing the bald man's beret. Is it that obvious? Oh, yes. The only thing that screams 'I am hiding hairlessness' more is a goatee.
jokes.setup: Homer thinking his beret disguises his baldness
jokes.punchline: Apu's brutally honest assessment of obvious baldness tells
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with good escalation - the goatee reference is a nice additional beat

jokes.text: A cowboy hat means 'I'm ashamed of my small penis.' Don't even ask me what this means! Yee-haw! I'm a-compensatin'!
jokes.setup: Homer considering a cowboy hat as baldness cover
jokes.punchline: Apu's increasingly explicit explanation of hat psychology
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective escalation joke with Apu's unexpected vulgarity

jokes.text: Come to kitchen and see picture of my dead wife. That's okay. I... No, no, no, no. She is not dead in picture. Uh... she is dying.
jokes.setup: Russian father inviting Marge to see wife's picture
jokes.punchline: The clarification that makes it worse - she's dying in the picture
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark misdirection - the 'correction' makes the situation more disturbing, not less

jokes.text: I'm sorry, did my voice go all evil? It is common with Russian accent.
jokes.setup: Making a somewhat sinister proposition
jokes.punchline: Self-awareness about how Russian accents sound menacing
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about accent stereotypes with nice self-awareness

jokes.text: Those two hairs were what was left of my youth, Moe.
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting his hair loss
jokes.punchline: The poetic overstatement about just two hairs
jokes.explanation: Nice poetic exaggeration with Homer's typical melodrama

jokes.text: Babar, king of the elephants. I read his books as a kid. He married his cousin, Celeste. That was my takeaway.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to think of sexy bald characters
jokes.punchline: His disturbing focus on the incestuous relationship in children's books
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark subversion - taking an innocent children's character and highlighting the weird family dynamics

jokes.text: We put two of those guys up there just for using a big word. 'Caché' is not a big word.
jokes.setup: Bullies punishing someone for using big words
jokes.punchline: Martin's correction using an even more pretentious word
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Martin's inability to help himself from being pretentious

jokes.text: Daddy said I was a accident.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation during driving lesson
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact delivery of traumatic family information
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with perfect economy and deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Every good booger deserves flicking.
jokes.setup: Learning musical notation mnemonic
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude adaptation of the memory device
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart juvenile humor - perfectly in character

jokes.text: Like Russian economy, under Putin. Stop making Putin jokes. You sound like police... under Putin.
jokes.setup: Making political jokes about Russia
jokes.punchline: The meta-joke about not being able to stop making Putin jokes
jokes.explanation: Decent political humor with self-awareness about the joke pattern

jokes.text: Finally. Someone who knows what a traffic stop is really all about. Uh, Chief, don't you think those jeans are a little tight? We're all not high-waisted like you, Lou, okay?
jokes.setup: Police corruption implications during traffic stop
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's defensive response about his fashion choices
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Wiggum's vanity and incompetence

jokes.text: Every note you play sounds like dying animal writhing on keys. Awesome!
jokes.setup: Harsh criticism of Bart's piano playing
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiasm for the violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's appreciation for destruction even in criticism

jokes.text: Buying a limo. He could tell big shots in the back that, no, they cannot smoke.
jokes.setup: Describing her father's dream
jokes.punchline: The modest nature of his power fantasy - just saying no to smoking
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - showing how small and specific immigrant dreams can be

jokes.text: I would love your hands forever.
jokes.setup: Zhenya being grateful for Bart's help
jokes.punchline: The unexpectedly intimate phrasing
jokes.explanation: Mildly uncomfortable romantic suggestion that feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: I would've been happy if Bart was just a piano mover.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing pride in Bart's musical performance
jokes.punchline: Her extremely low expectations for Bart's success
jokes.explanation: Good Marge moment showing her realistic but loving expectations for Bart

jokes.text: But no tickling the ivories. You treat them with respect.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony wanting piano lessons for his boys
jokes.punchline: The mobster's unexpectedly reverent attitude toward piano playing
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - tough mobster with refined musical sensibilities

jokes.text: Can you teach my monkey? Then I can deal with the Musician's Union instead of the Humane Society. Much easier to muscle.
jokes.setup: Request to teach a monkey piano
jokes.punchline: The casual admission of intimidating both unions and animal welfare groups
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor perfectly in character for Fat Tony - casual criminality with practical considerations

jokes.text: You lured Marge in with your hair. You trapped her with marriage. You skinned and field-gutted her by having kids. Now she's mounted on your wall for good, with fake glass eyes and a rubber tongue.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Homer 'caught' Marge
jokes.punchline: The extended hunting metaphor applied to marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and original extended metaphor comparing marriage to trophy hunting with vivid, disturbing imagery

jokes.text: No wife. I just slept with thousands of bald women.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about the man's wife after romantic advice
jokes.punchline: The reveal of a much more cynical romantic history
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expected romantic wisdom with dark humor and perfect economy

jokes.text: Unlike you, when you make your lumpy clam chowder. I guess Bart must be a musical genius, because frauds are always found out. Always.
jokes.setup: Helen making a seemingly supportive comment about Bart
jokes.punchline: The thinly veiled threat/prediction about frauds being exposed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Helen Lovejoy moment - passive-aggressive stirring disguised as support

jokes.text: Hurry up me on. I got to learn my mom's stripper music by midnight.
jokes.setup: Student wanting piano lessons
jokes.punchline: The disturbing family context for the musical education
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good economy, though somewhat gratuitous

jokes.text: Of course. But very, very, very angry at all the time I wasted.
jokes.setup: Bart asking if Marge would still be proud if he couldn't play piano
jokes.punchline: Marge's honest admission of how angry she'd be
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's honest feelings beneath her supportive facade

jokes.text: Hey, one of those notes could go to your brain and kill you. Are you sure you want to take that chance?
jokes.setup: Marge talking about notes going to her heart
jokes.punchline: Bart's medical warning about the dangers of musical notes
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic with Bart's attempt to discourage Marge's enthusiasm

jokes.text: First of all, you're over ten years old. Second, you're clearly Justin Bieber. That's another 25 bucks we'll never see.
jokes.setup: Judging a talent show contestant
jokes.punchline: The casual dismissal and monetary concern
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but fairly standard joke structure

jokes.text: Is it too late to make you breakfast in bed?
jokes.setup: Bart trying to make amends on Mother's Day
jokes.punchline: The pathetically inadequate gesture given the circumstances
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Bart's childish attempt at redemption

jokes.text: Did you pass? More or less. If I eat an orange wedge, the whole thing's gonna blow.
jokes.setup: Asking about passing the driver's test
jokes.punchline: The bizarre conditional nature of the 'pass'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about conditional success with nice economy

jokes.text: Even Russian dressing is just Thousand Island.
jokes.setup: Explaining that everyone in Russia succeeds by cheating
jokes.punchline: The food-based example of Russian deception
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor about cultural deception with good economy

jokes.text: Yeah, your generation won't have any dreams come true.
jokes.setup: Marge apologizing for forcing dreams on Bart
jokes.punchline: The casually devastating generational assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy - devastating social commentary delivered casually

jokes.text: Here's your new plate, Grampa. Oh, boy! There's crumbs on it!
jokes.setup: Bart delivering a plate of cookies to Grampa
jokes.punchline: Grampa's excitement about the crumbs rather than the cookies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Grampa's low expectations and simple pleasures

jokes.text: I've always wondered what you were hiding under your curly locks, my beautiful yellow melon.
jokes.setup: Homer revealing his bald head to Marge
jokes.punchline: Marge's sweet but weird description of his head as a 'yellow melon'
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with Marge's characteristic odd but loving imagery

tmdb_overview: Marge encourages Bart to explore his creative side, and his initial reticence quickly subsides when she hires Slava’s beautiful daughter, Zhenya, as his new piano instructor. Homer is shocked when he loses the last two hairs on his head, and employs a wide variety of headgear to try and keep his new found baldness a secret from Marge.
